englishDefinitions: (intransitive) To be prone on the ground.
(intransitive) To abase oneself before another person.
(intransitive) To be slavishly nice to someone or apologize in the hope of securing something.
(intransitive) To take pleasure in mundane activities.
persianTranslation: (ناگذر) خود را در برابر شخص دیگری تحقیر کردن.
(غیر گذرا) بردگی با کسی مهربان بودن یا عذرخواهی به امید تأمین چیزی.
(ناگذر) لذت بردن از فعالیت های پیش پا افتاده.
examples: She refused to grovel in front of the bully, standing her ground.
He had to grovel before his boss after missing the important meeting.
He hated to grovel, but he knew it was the only way to save his job.
